mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near zero (0.09%) since the COVID-19 policy response, the persistently low policy rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, providing a steady tailwind for S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon regardless of other conditions.
regime_hint: zero lower bound accommodative policy regime
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero-rate periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE of more than 1 percentage point over a two-month window signals a labor market recovery that boosts consumer income expectations and corporate earnings outlooks, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 1.0 percentage point over trailing 2 months
failure_condition: In periods where the UNRATE decline trigger fires, forward 60-day SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) across a majority of historical episodes since 1970.

mechanism_narrative: A joint rebound in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) indicates broad-based consumer and residential demand recovery that is not captured by either series alone; simultaneous month-over-month increases in both signal a durable demand-side recovery that lifts equities over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=449282 and HOUST=1009 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: consumer and housing demand rebound following shock
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce higher forward 20-day SPX returns than RSAFS momentum alone in an out-of-sample interaction test, indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: Following a sharp COVID-19-induced production collapse, a resumption of month-over-month growth in INDPRO signals reopening of manufacturing capacity and supply chains, which is priced by equity markets as a durable earnings-supportive signal over a quarterly horizon specifically during post-shock recovery regimes.
regime_hint: post-shock industrial recovery following COVID-19 lockdown
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month increases fail to correlate positively with forward 60-day SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.03) when measured specifically in post-recession recovery windows historically identified by NBER troughs.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) represent the fastest-moving confirmation of labor market healing after mass layoffs; a positive payroll print in the most recent release produces a short-horizon (one-week) positive reaction in S&P 500 prices as markets reprice recession risk downward.
regime_hint: any regime, amplified during sharp labor market dislocations
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month PAYEMS surprises are not followed by positive forward 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances since 1990, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth turns negative for two consecutive months, signaling deflationary pressure, markets interpret this as a sign of persistent demand weakness despite monetary easing, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as growth expectations deteriorate.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change negative for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered deflation-threshold instances historically, or the effect is not statistically significant at p < 0.10 across a 20-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS and RSAFS confirms that labor income gains are translating into actual consumer spending, a joint condition that is a stronger and more durable signal of real economic recovery than either series alone, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=140045 and RSAFS=449282 as of latest snapshot
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-RSAFS positive signal does not outperform a simple additive combination of the two single-series signals in out-of-sample forward return prediction (interaction term coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at the zero lower bound and INDPRO simultaneously begins expanding month-over-month, the combination signals that accommodative policy is successfully transmitting into real economic activity, an interaction that is particularly powerful for equities specifically during liquidity-trap recovery regimes, producing positive S&P 500 returns over one week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and INDPRO month-over-month growth positive
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.09 and INDPRO=106.6617 as of latest snapshot
regime_hint: zero lower bound liquidity trap recovery
failure_condition: The joint FEDFUNDS-INDPRO trigger fails to produce positive forward 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical zero-lower-bound episodes with rising INDPRO, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling windows within such episodes.
